response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web-focused developer with a broad polyglot background, but he does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role in any meaningful way. The most critical disqualifier is C++: the job requires 8 years of production C++ experience, yet the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components, and C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. His career trajectory is firmly rooted in TypeScript, Node.js, D-lang, and web technologies. He has touched distributed systems at Dunnhumby at scale, and has some AWS CDK exposure, but there is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform, managing infrastructure architecture, or holding a formal technical leadership role over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency design.
Consistent record of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks.
Broad multi-language and multi-domain experience (web, mobile, embedded, GIS) showing adaptability and engineering range.
concerns: C++ experience is essentially absent — roughly 6 months at National Instruments versus the required 8 years; C++ is not listed in his skills section at all.
No evidence of cloud platform ownership, infrastructure architecture leadership, or technical lead responsibilities over cloud or DevOps teams.
Career focus is overwhelmingly web/product-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, front-end frameworks) with no depth in compute infrastructure, storage systems, or hardware architecture.
